headline_marked: [[Australia 0/3]] after 2 overs, a first in ODI history as Bangladesh pacers dominate
headline: Australia 0/3 after 2 overs, a first in ODI history as Bangladesh pacers dominate
summary: Mustafizur Rahman took three wickets and Taskin Ahmed one as Australia lost four top-order batters inside the first 10 overs of the second ODI. This is the first time Australia has been 0/3 in ODIs.
details: Bangladesh pacers Mustafizur Rahman and Taskin Ahmed wreaked havoc on Australia in the second ODI, reducing them to 0/3 within the first two overs. This unprecedented collapse marks a historic low for the Australian team in One Day International history.

Taskin Ahmed struck first, dismissing opener Matthew Short for his third consecutive duck. Mustafizur Rahman then followed up by sending Cooper Connolly and Matt Renshaw back to the pavilion, leaving Australia reeling without a single run on the board.

Australia's woes continued as they lost their fourth wicket, Alex Carey, in the eighth over to Mustafizur Rahman. By the end of the 10th over, Australia had managed to score only 38 runs for the loss of four wickets, with Josh Inglis batting on 23.

This remarkable bowling performance comes after Bangladesh secured their first ODI win against Australia in 21 years in the previous match. Bangladesh is now aiming for their first-ever bilateral ODI series victory over the world champions.
The Tigers made one change to their winning lineup, bringing in Soumya Sarkar for Saif Hassan. Australia bolstered their pace attack with the inclusion of Riley Meredith.

The Mirpur pitch has shown assistance for pacers, similar to the first ODI where Nahid Rana was instrumental. Bangladesh's batting was anchored by Mosaddek Hossain's career-best 86 in the first match.
caption:
Australia collapses to 0/3 in the second ODI against Bangladesh, an unprecedented event in their ODI history. Mustafizur Rahman and Taskin Ahmed led the charge.

Bangladesh pacers Mustafizur Rahman and Taskin Ahmed wreaked havoc on Australia in the second ODI, reducing them to 0/3 within the first two overs. This unprecedented collapse marks a historic low for the Australian team in One Day International history.

Taskin Ahmed struck first, dismissing opener Matthew Short for his third consecutive duck. Mustafizur Rahman then followed up by sending Cooper Connolly and Matt Renshaw back to the pavilion, leaving Australia reeling without a single run on the board.

Australia's woes continued as they lost their fourth wicket, Alex Carey, in the eighth over to Mustafizur Rahman. By the end of the 10th over, Australia had managed to score only 38 runs for the loss of four wickets, with Josh Inglis batting on 23.

This remarkable bowling performance comes after Bangladesh secured their first ODI win against Australia in 21 years in the previous match. Bangladesh is now aiming for their first-ever bilateral ODI series victory over the world champions.

The Tigers made one change to their winning lineup, bringing in Soumya Sarkar for Saif Hassan. Australia bolstered their pace attack with the inclusion of Riley Meredith.

The Mirpur pitch has shown assistance for pacers, similar to the first ODI where Nahid Rana was instrumental. Bangladesh's batting was anchored by Mosaddek Hossain's career-best 86 in the first match.

#BANvsAUS #Cricket #ODI #BangladeshCricket #AUSvBAN

Source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star
tweet: Historic collapse! Australia are 0/3 after 2 overs in the 2nd ODI vs Bangladesh - a first in ODI history. Mustafizur Rahman and Taskin Ahmed on fire! #BANvsAUS #Cricket
source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star

headline_marked: PM [[unveils commemorative stamp]] for FIFA World Cup 2026
headline: PM unveils commemorative stamp for FIFA World Cup 2026
summary: A Tk 10 commemorative stamp and Tk 50 souvenir sheet were released to mark the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026. The event was attended by ministers and officials.
details: Prime Minister Tareq Rahman unveiled a Tk 10 commemorative stamp for the FIFA World Cup 2026. The event took place on Thursday morning at his office in the National Parliament Secretariat.
During the ceremony, a souvenir sheet valued at Tk 50 and a data card priced at Tk 5 were also released. A special seal was used for the occasion.

The unveiling was attended by Minister of Post, Telecommunication and Information Technology Fakir Mahbub Anam, Ministry Secretary Bilkis Jahan Rimi, Prime Minister's Additional Press Secretary Atiqur Rahman Rumen, and Director General of the Directorate of Posts S. M. Harunur Rashid.
caption:
To celebrate the FIFA World Cup 2026, a special Tk 10 commemorative stamp has been unveiled by Prime Minister Tareq Rahman.

Prime Minister Tareq Rahman unveiled a Tk 10 commemorative stamp for the FIFA World Cup 2026. The event took place on Thursday morning at his office in the National Parliament Secretariat.

During the ceremony, a souvenir sheet valued at Tk 50 and a data card priced at Tk 5 were also released. A special seal was used for the occasion.

The unveiling was attended by Minister of Post, Telecommunication and Information Technology Fakir Mahbub Anam, Ministry Secretary Bilkis Jahan Rimi, Prime Minister's Additional Press Secretary Atiqur Rahman Rumen, and Director General of the Directorate of Posts S. M. Harunur Rashid.

#FIFAWorldCup #Bangladesh #StampCollection #SportsNews #TareqRahman

Source: Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, Somoy News
